purpose: Representative case studies for hardening the UOGTO article and its supporting ontology analysis.

cases.title: Auction and mechanism design
cases.focus: Allocation rules, incentive compatibility, auction formats, and mechanism properties.
cases.why_it_matters: Stress-tests whether UOGTO captures strategic allocation, payments, and mechanism-level execution semantics rather than only abstract payoff relations.

cases.title: Voting and social choice
cases.focus: Preference aggregation, voting rules, collective choice, and social welfare criteria.
cases.why_it_matters: Checks whether UOGTO distinguishes individual strategy from collective decision rules and can represent outcome selection under institutional constraints.

cases.title: Security and Stackelberg games
cases.focus: Leader-follower commitment, defender-attacker roles, and adversarial strategic timing.
cases.why_it_matters: Tests whether UOGTO can model asymmetric information, commitment, and sequential best response without collapsing the domain into generic sequential play.

cases.title: Multi-agent reinforcement learning and Markov games
cases.focus: State transitions, policy learning, joint action spaces, and stochastic dynamics.
cases.why_it_matters: Validates that UOGTO can describe learning agents, environment state, and repeated interaction with traceable execution, not just static game forms.

cases.title: Agent-based policy simulation
cases.focus: Population heterogeneity, adaptive behavior, rule-driven interaction, and policy outcome analysis.
cases.why_it_matters: Separates agent-based simulation semantics from game-theoretic commitments while showing where overlap is legitimate and where alignment should remain external only.

cases.title: System-dynamics feedback game
cases.focus: Feedback loops, endogenous state evolution, policy levers, and long-run behavior.
cases.why_it_matters: Exercises the boundary between game-theoretic interaction and dynamic systems models so the article can justify what belongs in UOGTO and what belongs in adjacent modelling ontologies.

cases.title: LLM-agent and tool-use game
cases.focus: Agent tool selection, planner-executor feedback, prompt-mediated strategies, and observed action traces.
cases.why_it_matters: Captures emerging strategic interaction patterns in agentic systems and distinguishes them from general software workflows or provenance metadata.

cases.title: Executable trace and provenance
cases.focus: Run logs, decision traces, provenance capture, and reproducible execution evidence.
cases.why_it_matters: Provides the bridge from formal ontology modelling to the paper's evidence model by showing how executable artefacts support claims about game state, interaction history, and review decisions.
